title: The Leaping of the Forerunner
subtitle: Mary comes to the hill country, and the child in Elizabeth's womb leaps for joy

stillness: Before she went, Mary sat in the quiet of contemplation.
Prayer went before all her works.
Sit a little while as she did, in that same quiet, before you go anywhere.
gospel: Mary, rising up in those days, went into the hill country with haste,
And she entered into the house of Zacharias, and saluted Elizabeth.
And it came to pass, that when Elizabeth heard the salutation of Mary,
the infant leaped in her womb;
and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.
And she cried out with a loud voice, and said:
Blessed art thou among women, and blessed is the fruit of thy womb.
And whence is this to me, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?
On the eighth day they came to circumcise the child,
and his mother answering said: He shall be called John.
And his father wrote, saying: John is his name.
behold: Behold, then, how the Queen of heaven and earth goes —
not on horseback, but on foot,
with some of the maidens she kept at home,
along a road so long and so rough.
With her go modesty, humility, and poverty,
and the uprightness of every virtue.
With her too is the very Lord of hosts.
O how happy he would have been who had met Mary on that road,
and received from her the sound of her greeting!
Run, I beg you, run, mingle yourself with such joys;
fall down at the feet of them both,
and in the womb of the one embrace your Bridegroom,
and in the womb of the other honour His friend.
Attend here, and imitate this example of humility.
consider: As soon as the Virgin had greeted Elizabeth, John was filled with the Holy Spirit in the womb.
Feeling the presence of his Lord, for joy, in his mother's womb, he leaped and danced.
He moved within, as though longing to greet his Lord, and to rise up before Him,
as if he would come forth from the womb and run to meet Him.
What his tongue and voice could not do, he did with leaping mind and motion.
Already within his mother's body he seemed to cry: Behold the Lamb of God, behold Him who takes away the sins of the world.
Say, infant, say, greatest of all the prophets — whence is this exultation to you?
You are not yet born, and already you prophesy.
You know the coming of the Lord, and what you cannot do with your voice, leaping, you do what you can.
Yet Elizabeth cried out with a loud voice — not so much loud as devout;
for it is not the cry, but the love, that sounds in the ear of God.
prayer: you who were filled with God before you were born from the womb,
knowing God before you were known in the world:
to you I flee, anxious, whom grace made so dear a friend of God —
I, whom my own iniquity made so guilty before God.
Remember, then, lord, that as the grace of God so raised you up,
so your mercy may lift up the one whom his own fault has so cast down.
My iniquity made me what I am; but you, not you, but the grace of God made you.
Obtain for me, a wretch, that as you exulted with great joy at the first coming of our Saviour,
so I too may be counted worthy to rejoice at His second coming,
and to exult with the saints in glory. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Non clamor, sed amor sonat in aure Dei.
latinJewel.english: It is not the cry, but the love, that sounds in the ear of God.
